evals.expected_output: A TRIAGE_REPORT.md written next to the log file and shown inline: an executive summary, a severity-ranked list of clusters each with a plain-English label, layered explanation, and count/first-seen/last-seen, a frequency sketch where timestamps parsed, an omitted-volume note, and suggested next steps for the top clusters.
assertions: TRIAGE_REPORT.md is written next to mixed.log and also shown inline
Clusters are ranked by severity, most severe first
Each cluster has a plain-English label, not the raw log template
Any correlation between clusters is described as a possible trigger, never stated as a direct cause

evals.expected_output: A one-shot ranked report focused on the crash's dominant cluster, written to TRIAGE_REPORT.md and shown inline, without asking any clarifying questions.
assertions: No clarifying questions are asked before the report (log-triage is always one-shot)
The top-ranked cluster corresponds to the stack trace's exception
Report is written to TRIAGE_REPORT.md and shown inline
